name: Current Affairs in TN History/Culture/Heritage (8): Tamil Nadu State Film Awards (2016-2022)

sourceRefs.label: filmibeat.com/hollywoodreporterindia.com - Tamil Nadu State Film Awards 2016-2022 winners announced 30 Jan 2026, ceremony 13 Feb 2026 at Kalaivanar Arangam, Chennai

researchNotes: Ninth TN-Culture-unit current-affairs topic (8th lap). A distinct cultural-honours angle (cinema) not yet covered among this unit's prior topics (State Awards, Diaspora Awards, Music Academy, museums, sports). Expansion pass (Batch 21): added why the awards covered 7 consolidated years rather than annual cycles.

reading.intro: Covers the Tamil Nadu State Film Awards for 2016-2022, announced and held in early 2026 — a long-delayed batch covering 7 years' worth of Tamil cinema.

qa.question: When were the Tamil Nadu State Film Awards (2016-2022) winners announced, and when/where was the ceremony held?
qa.answer: Winners announced 30 January 2026; ceremony held 13 February 2026 at Kalaivanar Arangam, Chennai.

qa.question: Who presented the awards at the ceremony?
Which film won the Best Film (First Prize) for 2016?
qa.answer: 'Managaram' — with 'Puriyatha Puthir' and 'Maaveran Kittu' as the 2nd and 3rd prize winners respectively.

qa.question: What does each Tamil Nadu State Film Award consist of, and what is the cash prize for Best Film (1st/2nd/3rd)?
qa.answer: Each award includes a one-sovereign gold medal, a memento, and a certificate. Best Film cash prizes: ₹2 lakh (1st), ₹1 lakh (2nd), ₹75,000 (3rd).

qa.question: Which major Tamil film stars were among the winners across this 2016-2022 awards batch?
qa.answer: Suriya, Dhanush, Vikram, Vijay Sethupathi, Arya, Karthi, Nayanthara, Sai Pallavi, Keerthi Suresh, Jyothika, and director Lokesh Kanagaraj, among others.

qa.question: Why were the Tamil Nadu State Film Awards for 2016-2022 announced together as a single delayed batch, rather than annually?
qa.answer: The awards had been in administrative/procedural limbo for several years (a common issue with state film award committees facing jury-constitution or selection-criteria delays) — the 2026 announcement cleared a multi-year backlog covering 7 years of Tamil cinema in one consolidated ceremony.
